problem: Our water polo team has 15 members.  I want to choose a starting team consisting of 7 players, one of whom will be the goalie (the other six positions are interchangeable, so the order in which they are chosen doesn't matter).  In how many ways can I choose my starting team?

classified_questions.question: What is the first step in choosing the starting team?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How many options do we have for choosing the goalie out of the 15 members?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: After choosing the goalie, how many players are left to choose from for the remaining 6 positions?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How many ways are there to choose the first player after selecting the goalie?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can you explain why we multiply the number of ways to choose each player together?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Why do we divide by 6! (6 factorial) in the final calculation?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can you think of any other scenarios where we might need to use a similar approach to counting combinations?
classified_questions.label: e

classified_questions.question: Can you generalize the formula for choosing a starting team with any number of players and positions?
classified_questions.label: e
